parameters.text:
=Analyze this email including any forwarded or quoted content in the body. Extract two things:
1. Any appointment or meeting with a specific proposed date and time
2. Any todo item worth tracking — but be selective

If the email is a forwarded message, analyze the full original conversation, not just the forwarding note.

Today's date: {{ new Date().toLocaleDateString('de-DE', {day:'2-digit', month:'2-digit', year:'numeric'}) }}
From: {{ $json.from }}
Subject: {{ $json.subject }}
Date: {{ $json.date }}
Body (may contain forwarded messages):
{{ $json.text || $json.snippet || '' }}

IMPORTANT: Write ALL output text fields in German (title, description, todoTitle, todoSubItems).
IMPORTANT: Always use the full year (e.g. 2026) in the date field. If no year is mentioned in the email, use the year from Today's date above.

Return ONLY this JSON (no markdown, no code blocks):
{
  "hasAppointment": true or false,
  "title": "calendar event title",
  "date": "YYYY-MM-DD or empty",
  "startTime": "HH:MM in 24h or empty",
  "endTime": "HH:MM in 24h or empty",
  "description": "short appointment description or empty",
  "location": "location or empty",
  "hasTodo": true or false,
  "todoTitle": "single concise todo item, emoji-prefixed, max 100 chars",
  "todoSubItems": []
}

Rules for hasAppointment:
- ONLY true when a specific date is explicitly proposed or confirmed

Rules for hasTodo:

SKIP (false) when:
- The email was written/sent by me (check From field — if it is my own address, skip)
- HTW Berlin: social events, job postings, student club events, general announcements, promotional emails, surveys
- Pure newsletters, marketing, or promotional content
- Automated system notifications with no personal action needed

YES (true) for:
- HTW Berlin ONLY: course cancellation, hybrid/online mode change, assignment deadline, exam registration, grade entry, enrollment/re-registration deadline
- Shipping / delivery: order shipped, tracking number, delivery expected
- Invoice or receipt: payment due, subscription renewed, order confirmed
- Reservation confirmed: hotel, flight, restaurant, event ticket
- Reply explicitly requested or clearly needed
- Registration / application with deadline
- Login credentials or access data received
- Contract, document, or important file to review/sign

Rules for todoTitle:
- One self-contained phrase, emoji-prefixed, no separate description
- Include key details inline: amount, date, tracking number, sender name
- Emoji guide: 📦 shipping, 🧾 invoice/receipt, 📅 appointment, ↩ reply needed, ✅ confirmation, ⚠️ deadline, 🔑 credentials, 📝 document
- Examples: "📦 Paket: Amazon – AirPods (DE123456789)", "🧾 Rechnung: Spotify 9,99€", "⚠️ HTW: Abgabe Analysis-Hausarbeit bis 20.06."

Rules for todoSubItems:
- Empty [] for simple emails
- Only fill when there are 2+ genuinely distinct follow-up steps
- Each sub-item: short actionable phrase
